meta.name: Aswin Pradeep C
meta.tagline: Engineer by obsession. SWE, cyclist, self-hoster, occasional open source contributor.

now: Digging into how things work at scale, building and breaking things to understand the process in depth. Having fun extending engineering into daily life. Recently built Picnix to make trip planning less painful. Writing about backend performance, LLM systems, and whatever else I learn along the way.
about: I'm a backend engineer from Kerala. I write Python — FastAPI, Django, Postgres, Redis — and whatever the problem actually needs.

Most of my real learning comes from my work as a backend engineer, my college life at CUSAT where I tried building a startup and led tech clubs, and from freelancing and internships along the way. I organized Make-A-Ton 7.0, South India's largest hackathon — equal parts chaos and genuinely fun.
I have contributed to devb.io, an open-source developer bio platform, which made top 10 at FOSSHack 2025. I self-host Ente Photos with redundant backups and run a few other experiments on my homelab.
If I'm not at the laptop, I'm on a cycle 🚴

bullets: Built agentic pipelines using LangGraph — featuring autonomous tool-calling, sandboxed code execution, and self-correction loops for multi-step task completion.

Cut latency on a CPU-bound dynamic pricing API from 9s to under 2s: profiled hotspots, parallelised computation with ProcessPoolExecutor, batched I/O with asyncio.gather, and added HTTP connection pooling, Redis caching, and exponential backoff with jitter.
Developed an LLM-based search system with RAG architecture for enhanced financial data retrieval.
Automated and optimized manual processes with machine learning to improve efficiency.
Built RESTful APIs for the travel module with input validation and error handling.
Reduced response times significantly on high-traffic endpoints using Redis caching.
Proposed and developed proof-of-concept implementations for AI-powered workflow optimizations.
Built a prototype using OpenCV for automated coupon code extraction, using region-of-interest detection to filter out irrelevant text and streamline water delivery operations.

projects.desc: Academic project management system with role-based access, plagiarism checking, team management, document uploads, and support ticket system.
